sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for the Virtual Management of Acne
sources.population: Adolescents and adults with acne complaints, including post-adolescent acne.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth acne documentation; treatment selections remain clinician-entered and pending review.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment and Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers distribution, lesion types, scarring, hyperpigmentation, hormonal patterns, pain, prior treatment, and psychological impact.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Clinical Assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies lesion type and severity, skin type, scarring, hyperpigmentation, menstrual or androgen context, products, medicines, treatment history, and psychological impact.

exact_sections.heading: 8.1 Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists scarring risk, depression or severe psychosocial impact, treatment non-response, endocrine concern, and severe or nodulocystic acne.

exact_sections.heading: 8.2 Treatment Principles; 9. Treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Provides clinician-led management, adherence, skin-care, medicine, and review context; dose-bearing details are not mapped.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15
sources.population: Adults presenting with acute low back pain.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for acute adult low-back-pain telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for chronic pain, in-person examination, and settings outside Dubai.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5–6 Virtual assessment and clinical history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.

exact_sections.heading: 8.13 Cauda equina syndrome and 10.3 Imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes cauda-equina symptom combinations and limits imaging in short-duration low back pain without associated concern features.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis
sources.population: People with acute rhinosinusitis symptoms; chronic, invasive fungal, and complicated disease require different pathways.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth acute-rhinosinusitis documentation; partial for all chronic sinus and facial-pain causes.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Types; 8. Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines duration patterns and lists congestion, discharge, postnasal drip, dental discomfort, facial pain or pressure, fever, fatigue, cough, smell change, and headache.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.

exact_sections.heading: 12. Investigations; 13. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Limits imaging and other tests to clinician-selected contexts and lists emergency, specialist, systemic-illness, and non-response referral criteria.

exact_sections.heading: 14. Management
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Provides clinician-led conservative and pharmacological management choices; no dose-bearing content is mapped into this project.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
sources.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
sources.clinical_setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 1, 5, 6 and 9 — risks, morphology, distribution, symptoms, diagnosis, and history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7 and 13 — red flags and referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 11–12 — clinician-led avoidance, skin care, and treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis
sources.population: Adults and children presenting with allergic-rhinitis complaints; age-specific medicine details are not mapped.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth allergic-rhinitis documentation; partial for broad nasal obstruction and non-allergic causes.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment; 6. Clinical History/Symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Management; 12. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
sources.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment; Common Features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Management
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
sources.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — mechanisms and burn classification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — airway, vital signs, depth, site, size, and total body surface area
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7–9 — red flags and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led burn care, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, monitoring, and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
sources.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

exact_sections.heading: Section 6 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression
sources.population: Adults presenting with suspected or established depressive symptoms; emergency, severe, psychotic, bipolar, or safeguarding presentations require escalation.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth depression documentation and risk screening; treatment, diagnosis, and local emergency pathways remain clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — depressive features and structured assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

exact_sections.heading: Differential diagnosis — adjustment disorder with depressed mood
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes depressed mood in response to an identifiable psychosocial stressor and supports documenting single, multiple, recurrent, or continuing social and occupational stressors.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management, monitoring, and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysmenorrhoea – 26
sources.population: Adults presenting with dysmenorrhoea complaints.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult dysmenorrhoea documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for adolescents, face-to-face examination, pregnancy complications, and non-Dubai settings.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment and Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the Dubai telehealth setting and menstrual, pain, associated symptom, functional impact, contraception, medical, surgical, and sexual history required for dysmenorrhoea assessment.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags and exclusion of secondary dysmenorrhoea
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sudden or constant severe pain, peritonism, fever, abnormal bleeding or discharge, instability, anaemia, weight loss, infertility, dyspareunia, non-midline pain, and progressive symptoms as concern features.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Management — non-pharmacological measures and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes lifestyle measures and follow-up after teleconsultation; medication doses were reviewed but are not mapped into the workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines emergency and specialist referral contexts for sudden or severe pain, peritonism, pregnancy concern, abnormal bleeding, fever or discharge, progressive symptoms, and suspected secondary pathology.

sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
sources.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Scope, purpose, and applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth dysuria scope and qualified applicability.

exact_sections.heading: Clinical history for dysuria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

exact_sections.heading: Red flags and referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

exact_sections.heading: Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

sources.exact_document_title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization
sources.population: Children, adolescents, and adults receiving immunization services, including the national childhood programme.
sources.clinical_setting: All DHA-licensed public and private healthcare settings in Dubai.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for immunization-service documentation in Dubai; partial for a generic child vaccination workflow outside Dubai or beyond the reviewed sections.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5. Childhood Immunization Program 0 to 6 Years
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA immunization scope and the national childhood programme and schedule context.

exact_sections.heading: 5.8 Catch-Up Immunization for Children Aged 4 Months Through 6 Years
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires review of the child vaccination history, use of age-appropriate catch-up scheduling, and completion without restarting a series solely because of elapsed time.

exact_sections.heading: 13. Recommendation Nine: Contraindications and Precautions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires screening before each dose and identifies severe previous reaction, acute illness severity, and immunosuppression-related precautions.

exact_sections.heading: 15. Recommendation Eleven: Vaccine Administration and Recording — consent, preparation, screening, and interview questions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines consent context, scheduled-dose and history checks, illness, previous severe reaction, immune status, medication and recent-vaccine screening.

exact_sections.heading: 15.8 Patient Education
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires benefits and risks discussion and after-care information on common effects, when to seek attention, and when to notify the facility.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
sources.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags and evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — substances, medicines, comorbidity, environment, and selected tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21
sources.population: People receiving laboratory monitoring for listed chronic conditions, including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hyperlipidaemia.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for listed chronic-condition laboratory monitoring in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad follow-up and arbitrary laboratory-result workflows.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background; 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines medication and report verification, DHA telehealth scope, emergency exclusions, and laboratory-monitoring purpose.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Chronic conditions that could be managed virtually
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — required laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists condition-linked baseline and follow-up laboratory monitoring and states that unlisted testing remains clinician-directed.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — statin monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Links statin review to documented ALT and cholesterol or lipid results before clinician prescribing decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — antithyroid and thyroxine therapy monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
sources.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — mechanism, symptoms, and examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7–9 — imaging and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-determined CT, MRI, or X-ray documentation and immediate referral or escalation when serious-injury criteria are present.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — observation, advice, return precautions, and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47
sources.population: People with muscle sprains or strains suitable for virtual assessment.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for sprain- or strain-compatible injury documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad foot, hand, sports, fracture, operative, or diagnosis-specific workflows.

exact_sections.heading: 1–6 Background, scope, applicability, causes, and risk factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

exact_sections.heading: 7 Symptoms and signs; 7.4 Relevant history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

exact_sections.heading: 8 Red flags; 9 Differential diagnosis; 10 Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

exact_sections.heading: 11 Management; 12 Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Neck Pain – 22
sources.population: Adults presenting with neck pain.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult neck-pain telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for in-person examination, paediatric patients, and non-Dubai settings.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Evaluation and 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires red-flag identification and lists myelopathic symptoms, fever, immunosuppression, weight loss, cancer, systemic symptoms, chest symptoms, and significant trauma.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Clinical History and face-to-face examination elements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports onset, duration, pain character, arm radiation, paraesthesia, activity impact, posture, movement, range of motion, palpation, neurological assessment, gait, and provocative-manoeuvre documentation.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Investigation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: States that atraumatic neck pain without red flags generally needs no imaging and reserves radiography, CT, or MRI for red flags, progressive neurology, or persistent moderate-to-severe pain.

exact_sections.heading: 12. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists trauma, persistent symptoms, headache, numbness or tingling, lower-limb weakness, gait or coordination issues, bowel or bladder dysfunction, immunosuppression, weight loss, fever, and chest symptoms.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28
sources.population: Adults with osteoarthritis symptoms, particularly middle-aged and older adults.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for osteoarthritis-compatible knee and hip documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for undifferentiated joint pain, acute injury, and settings outside Dubai.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags and 7. Clinical Manifestation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.

exact_sections.heading: 8.1.4 Knee and 8.1.5 Hip
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Diagnosis and Imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.

exact_sections.heading: 11. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis
sources.population: Infants, adolescents, and adults with suspected or clinician-diagnosed seborrheic dermatitis, with differential and referral qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face or dermatology referral where indicated.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for selected seborrheic-dermatitis symptoms, distribution, appearance, red flags, differential testing, and documentation-only management fields. Diagnosis, medicine choice, dose, treatment instruction, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 1 and 5 — background, clinical history, distribution, course, and diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

exact_sections.heading: Section 6 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — differential diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Section 8 — clinician-led advice and pharmacological treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting skin-care, dandruff-shampoo, eyelid-care, clinician-stated topical treatment, and follow-up discussion without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Swollen Eyelid – 40
sources.population: People with eyelid swelling, including stye, chalazion, blepharitis, allergy, infection, trauma, and lesion differentials, with telehealth exclusions.
sources.clinical_setting: DHA-licensed telehealth services with face-to-face or specialist referral where indicated.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for selected Dubai telehealth stye, blepharitis, eyelid-feature, and referral documentation. Diagnosis, medicine choice, dose, treatment instruction, procedure, urgency conclusion, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 1.2 and 5.1 — common eyelid symptoms and styes
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented eyelid irritation, redness, swelling, discharge, pain context, stye or hordeolum identity, typical eyelid-lump appearance, and selected risk-factor fields.

exact_sections.heading: Section 5.3 — blepharitis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, gritty, burning, stinging, greasy, itchy, swollen or crusted eyelids, light sensitivity, eyelid observation, and clinician-documented eyelid-hygiene discussion.

exact_sections.heading: Section 9 — referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral, worsening, vision change, severe redness, severe pain, photophobia, spread, trauma, uncertain diagnosis, and refractory-symptom documentation without automatically concluding urgency.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11
sources.population: Adults presenting with abdominal pain.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth abdominal-pain documentation; partial where the baseline workflow has no adult-only filter or telehealth restriction.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — clinical history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

exact_sections.heading: 11. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
sources.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Clinical symptoms and 6. Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

exact_sections.heading: 11.8 Uncontrolled asthma and 12. Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

exact_sections.evidence_summary: Limits testing to the assessed presentation and lists blood studies, chest radiography, ECG, and echocardiography where clinically indicated.

sources.exact_document_title: Antenatal Care Standard
sources.population: Pregnant women and adolescent girls receiving pre-conception or antenatal care.

sources.applicability_note: Direct for routine antenatal documentation in Abu Dhabi; partial for high-risk or complication-specific care and for use outside Abu Dhabi.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Standard Scope and Purpose; 3.4 Antenatal Care; 3.4.1 Routine Antenatal Care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — Routine Antenatal Screening and Care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies gestation-linked weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal heart, fundal height, fetal movement, laboratory, ultrasound, glucose-screening, counselling, and follow-up documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — Criteria for High-Risk Pregnancies
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines conditions that require additional obstetric review and preserves escalation as a clinician decision rather than an automatic workflow output.

sources.exact_document_title: Standard for Day Surgery/Procedure Service
sources.population: Patients receiving planned day surgery or procedure services in DoH-licensed facilities, with age, risk, procedure, anesthesia, and discharge qualifications.
sources.clinical_setting: DoH-licensed day surgery and procedure facilities in Abu Dhabi.
sources.applicability_note: UAE-direct for service governance, assessment, care plans, pre/intra/post-procedure documentation, records, discharge, and patient education. It does not replace anesthesiologist judgment, facility policy, consent, credentialing, or emergency care.

exact_sections.heading: Scope, definitions, and service requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the licensed day-surgery and procedure service, eligible service scope, and facility responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Governance, workforce, policies, and risk controls
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting governance, credentialing, safety, escalation, infection-control, medication, emergency, and quality policy context without generating a clinical action.

exact_sections.heading: Patient assessment and individualized care plan
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting pre-procedure assessment, history, examination, risk, suitability, investigations, consent, and an individualized clinician-entered care plan.

exact_sections.heading: Pre-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting identity, procedure, site, consent, fasting, preparation, medicines, allergy, and pre-procedure checks when assessed or completed.

exact_sections.heading: Intra-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports procedure, monitoring, equipment, staff, events, and clinical-record documentation during a procedure without directing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recovery and discharge requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recovery observation, pain or nausea assessment, discharge criteria, instructions, escort, complications, handover, and follow-up only as clinician-completed documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Health-record requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports complete, contemporaneous records for assessment, consent, anesthesia or sedation, procedure, monitoring, recovery, discharge, complications, and follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Patient education and communication
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient-centred explanation, understandable language, interpreter support, written information, questions, and documented education.

sources.applicability_note: Direct for age-specific well-child screening in Abu Dhabi; partial for children outside the stated age range and for symptom-led growth or developmental presentations. The published copy states an effective date of TBD.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Standard Scope; 3.1 History Components; 3.2 Comprehensive Physical Examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the 1-month-to-6-year population and requires parent history, age-specific physical examination, vital signs, developmental milestones, screening, vaccination discussion, and EMR documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — Child Screening Requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies age-linked weight, length or height, head circumference, growth plotting, feeding and medical history, developmental milestones, screening, immunization review, and caregiver counselling.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 5 — Developmental Delays Screening; Appendix 6 — Normal Developmental Milestones
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines developmental and autism screening prompts, parent concern, walking, speech and social domains, and referral pathways for concerning screening results.

exact_sections.heading: 4. Key Stakeholder Roles and Responsibilities
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires effective record keeping and documentation of screening findings, caregiver education, consent, and refusal.

sources.issuing_organisation: United Arab Emirates Ministry of Health and Prevention
sources.exact_document_title: Attestation of Medical Leaves and Reports
sources.version: MOHAP service code 110-10-019-000; webpage last updated 10 July 2026
sources.jurisdiction: United Arab Emirates federal service, with competent-emirate authority qualifications
sources.population: Patients of all ages submitting a medical leave, companion leave, or medical report for attestation.
sources.clinical_setting: Administrative attestation of documents issued by licensed UAE health facilities or appropriately certified foreign institutions; not a clinical assessment standard.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for document type, licensed-facility, attestation, duration-band and medical-committee requirements. It does not define clinical incapacity, fitness, diagnosis disclosure, assessment content, dates, duration, backdating, or school-note requirements.

exact_sections.heading: About this service; required documents; target audience
exact_sections.locator: official service page sections “About this service”, “Required documents”, and “Target audience”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that an issued sick-leave certificate or related medical report is being submitted for employer-facing attestation and distinguishes sick leave, companion leave and medical reports without establishing clinical incapacity.

exact_sections.heading: Conditions and requirements
exact_sections.locator: official service page section “Conditions and requirements”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports licensed-facility, competent-authority, issuance-to-attestation interval, duration-band and medical-committee approval context; it does not authorize automatic certification, backdating, duration selection, or diagnosis disclosure.

sources.issuing_organisation: Ministry of Health and Prevention, United Arab Emirates
sources.exact_document_title: Practical Guideline for Management of Tobacco Dependence
sources.population: People who use tobacco, recently stopped tobacco, do not currently use tobacco, or may be exposed to second-hand smoke.
sources.clinical_setting: Clinical and public-health tobacco-use identification, assessment, cessation support, and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Direct UAE source for documenting tobacco status, exposure, quit readiness, prior attempts, barriers, clinician-discussed support, referral, and follow-up; medicine names, doses, and autonomous cessation instructions are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Who Can Use this Guide and Basic and Essential Facts
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports healthcare-provider use, tobacco-status identification and documentation, cessation support, and second-hand-smoke exposure context.

exact_sections.heading: The 5 As and Identification and Assessment of Tobacco Use Status
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.

exact_sections.heading: Assist and Arrange follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented counselling, support, pharmacotherapy discussion, referral, and arranged follow-up without mapping medicine names or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Former tobacco users, relapse potential, and behavioural intervention summary
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recent abstinence, remaining challenges, relapse risk, prior success, repeated intervention, and follow-up.
